claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch system implements provider-specific configuration with boundary enforcement and tool capability preservation for Claude, Codex, and custom providers.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime executes evidence agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) with provider-specific config and boundary auditing.
evidence.why: Core evidence dispatch implementation that spawns provider agents and validates boundary constraints.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs provider-specific CLI arguments for Claude, Codex, and custom providers with isolated model and sandbox config.
evidence.why: Provider invocation logic prevents cross-provider configuration leakage.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots detect project file mutations before/after evidence agent execution using git-backed file tracking.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement detects any project mutations outside allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentProvider maps provider aliases (anthropic→claude, openai→codex) to normalized provider names.
evidence.why: Provider normalization prevents provider name drift between config and execution.

evidence.summary: buildAgentAdapterCapabilities defines provider-specific capabilities including sandbox mode, approval policy, file_write, and shell_exec per provider.
evidence.why: Provider capabilities contract documents which features each provider supports.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agent_tool_profile (boundary/research/full) and resets Claude settings, tools, and session persistence for evidence mode.
evidence.why: Evidence mode configuration isolates tool capabilities based on authorized profile level.

evidence.summary: Test verifies codex dispatch does not inherit claude default model or force read-only sandbox.
evidence.why: Evidence that provider-specific model config is isolated between providers.

evidence.summary: Test verifies claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags and relies on boundary enforcement instead of CLI tool filtering.
evidence.why: Evidence that tool capability preservation uses boundary enforcement rather than provider-side restrictions.

evidence.summary: Test verifies claude full tool profile requires explicit allowFullAgentTools authorization; defaults to boundary profile without authorization.
evidence.why: Evidence that full tool capabilities require explicit opt-in rather than being default.

evidence.summary: Test suite verifies boundary detection blocks project file mutations even when agent results pass, including git-backed detection of untracked and gitignored files.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary enforcement prevents target project mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary audit compares before/after snapshots and adds BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for any detected project changes.
evidence.why: Boundary audit converts any detected mutations into PRD readiness blockers.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier protocols with writes_code=false and evidence scope requirements.
evidence.why: Evidence agent protocol definition documents read-only constraint and scope validation requirements.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness validates evidence agent results, checks scope declarations, and blocks PRD readiness when project facts lack project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Readiness enforcement separates assumptions from facts and requires project evidence for project claims.

assumptions: Git-based boundary detection works correctly in the target project repository
Provider CLI commands (claude, codex) are available and correctly versioned in the execution environment
risks: A bug in boundary snapshot logic could fail to detect project mutations, allowing silent evidence-agent mutations
Provider-specific model config normalization could drift if new providers are added without updating PROVIDER_ALIASES
Custom provider adapter executes arbitrary commands without sandbox verification by the SDK

result.notes: Evidence dispatch system implements provider-isolated configuration with boundary enforcement and tool capability preservation. All key claims verified from source code and tests.
